title: The Best Mole Hole Ever
summary: Papa Mole builds a grand new tunnel home, enlisting his family to sweep out over 1,120 pieces of dirt before decorating with windows, stairs, and furniture. Exhausted by their efforts, the entire mole family, including Mama and Grandma Mole, quickly falls into a deep slumber. Their peaceful rest is eventually interrupted by the arrival of Gagok, a magic paper bird, and a flock of chickadees. After sharing a breakfast of twigs, worms, and ants, the birds also succumb to sleep right on the kitchen table, leading to much laughter when the moles wake up. The group then contemplates a journey to the Crystal Mountain to ensure the paper birds can find more magic crystals.

highlightQuote.text: I got out your brooms, now sweep! There's 11, 20 dirt in the rooms.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding dong, ding dong.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And who is it? said the Mystic Moles.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's your best grown-up Papa Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh hi Papa Mole, good morning. What you up to today?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Making a new tunnel home just for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, for us? Thank you. What's going to be in the new tunnel?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Could you help me? Could you help me?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, we can all dig together, said Disco. And Disco said, 'Yeah me too, I'll dig with you.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then we have to add the rooms and sweep the dirt out of them.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We sure do. Let's get started. Where's this new hole you're digging for us, Papa Mole?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Made made made, I made all the rooms so now Disco and Disco are you really good at sweeping?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Um, yes, we love sweeping.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So then, I got out your brooms, now sweep! There's 11, 20 dirt in the rooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, is that a lot?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, that's a lot of dirt.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, we better get sweeping. And Disco got a broom, and Disco got a broom, and they both got dustpans, and they set to work sweeping all the dirt out of the rooms in their new tunnel. Sweep sweep sweep sweep sweep sweep sweep. And Disco said, 'I've been sweeping forever! Am I almost done?' And Disco said, 'Keep going, this is what Papa Mole told us to do.' And Disco said, 'Oh, maybe you're right. I think we're done.' And so they finished sweeping.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Papa Mole, are we done?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Just two more, just one more piece of dirt left. Yes, said Papa Mole, just sweep that one more piece and then we'll be done. And now we can do the rest of our setup for our rooms. What comes next, do you think? Should we add furniture, or put in some windows, or stairs?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I added windows and stairs, now we gotta add what? I added the lights, the lamps. What else did you say?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Maybe some house plants?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Plants? What else did you say?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Furniture.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Furniture! Yeah, what are you gonna add for furniture?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Beds.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bookcase.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mhm. What else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Walls. Actually walls aren't furniture.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's true. But we could put shelves on the wall and then put things on the shelves.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: B-b-b-bed! And a bed. Table, chairs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Should we have some pictures on the walls?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dresser, closet.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dollhouse, game.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Door!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: This is gonna be the best mole hole ever.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Done! I put the things through the floor and they do mischief with things to play with. I think we're set.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, fantastic. Now, let's try it out, said Disco. I'm a little tired from all this hard work setting up our new mole hole, so I'm just gonna lie down on the bed for a second and see if I... zzz. And Disco said, 'Hey Disco, you fell right asleep.' And Disco said, 'Yeah, so sleepy... zzz.' And Disco said, 'Well, I guess I better see if my bed is okay because I'm not sure if it's soft enough for me to... zzz.' And Papa Mole said, 'Disco, Disco, you both fell asleep! I'm just gonna sit down here for a second in this chair and then... zzz.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Mama Mole says, 'This chair is really comfy... zzz.' And Grandma Mole says, 'This chair looks... zzz. This chair looks... zzz. This chair looks... zzz.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so all the moles fell asleep in their beautiful new mole hole.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then they woke up!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they woke up and they heard someone knocking at the door. And who was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they went down and they said, 'It's nobody!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what was that knocking sound?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was the magic bird, it was the paper bird that came back.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the paper bird's name I think was Gagok.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Gagok. And he came back and said, 'My crystal that helps me fly... the crystal mountain isn't getting eaten.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's good. I'm glad to hear that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So we can go there as long as there's a tweet-tweet, tweet!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What does that mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's another Gagok bird flying. He says he has his crystal because he gulped it and now he's flying.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then they heard a chickadee-dee-dee, chickadee-dee-dee.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who's that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's a chickadee.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi chickadee.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Chickadee-dee-dee... sang all the chickadees.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi chickadees.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cheep cheep! went the chicks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Papa Mole said, 'Excuse me birds,' because now there were quite a few birds at the door to their new house. 'Would you like to visit our mole hole and come inside and have a little breakfast with us?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah! Munch! I ate all the wor... I ate all the twigs, we ate a few of the worms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, good.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We ate a bit of the ants and then that was very delicious and then they said 'zzz' we just fell asleep.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the birds fell asleep in the mole's house? Oh my goodness.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then they woke up and heard, 'What's all the commotion?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What's all the commotion?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And they went and they walked into the living room and they got Disco and Disco and Papa Mole laughing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What's so funny, moles? What's so funny?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A kids' story and then find out... well they thought it was so funny that all the birds came into their house to visit and they just had a little snack and then 'zzz' they all fell asleep right on the kitchen table.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's really funny! But what else did and then they went 'zzz' and then the birds went flap-flap-flap! And they flew out the door and then and then the birds said, 'Where's our crystals?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, maybe we could get more from the Crystal Mountain, said Papa Mole.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Crunch! Just there's no... that wasn't a crunch from Crystal Mountain. It was crunch from somewhere else.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Somebody crunching an acorn maybe?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A squirrel! It was probably a squirrel, that's true. Probably a squirrel. And then a chipmunk, a chipmunk was just crunching on a walnut.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yum. Crunchy walnut.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right Iz, let's have one last part of the story. Should all everyone, the birds and the mole together go and visit the Crystal Mountain?

characters.name: Papa Mole
characters.description: The wise leader and industrious architect of the Mischief Moles, this polite elder visionary guides his kin from the attic’s shadowed heights toward the stars and across biting, crystalline ice. A master pathfinder who initiated their voyage upon unearthing an ancient tome, he balances the weight of celestial navigation with the tactile craft of carving a new tunnel home and the thoughtful devotion of planning whimsical days of activities for his children. Whether introducing surprise robots for play, anchoring meditations during a frozen trek, or sleep-sitting in his favorite chair, his tactical brilliance ensures their furthest discoveries remain rooted in the rhythmic, quiet peace of a sanctuary built by his own seasoned hands.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Second Disco
characters.description: Another mole character named Disco who assists in digging and sweeping.

characters.name: Mama Mole
characters.description: A mindful member of the mole family who guides Disco on contemplative nature walks along the sidewalk’s edge, illuminating their path with a steadying headlamp. Pairing firm yet nurturing instructions with vital maintenance exercises to ensure the robot’s long-term mechanical well-being, they eventually retreat from the outdoors to join the communal deep sleep in the new living room.

characters.name: Grandma Mole
characters.description: An elder mole who tries out various chairs and promptly falls asleep.

characters.name: Gagok
characters.description: A magical paper bird and loyal companion to Seeker, this enchanted creature requires the essence of a specialized crystal to take wing and navigate the skies.

characters.name: Chickadee
characters.description: A small bird that visits the mole hole for a breakfast of twigs and worms.

characters.name: Squirrel
characters.description: A fuzzy gray creature with a voluminous, silver tail, once only a phantom heard crunching on neighborhood acorns, now reveals itself as it forages for nuts with jittery grace.

characters.name: Chipmunk
characters.description: A small creature heard crunching on a walnut near the Crystal Mountain.

places.name: Mole Hole
places.description: A newly constructed tunnel home featuring windows, stairs, and many cozy chairs.

places.name: Crystal Mountain
places.description: The caretaker’s secluded dwelling serves as a hallowed sanctuary where magical birds descend to harvest the luminous crystals essential for their flight.

places.name: Living Room
places.description: Tucked within the mole hole’s earthy embrace, this cozy sanctuary is anchored by overstuffed chairs and the lingering warmth of shared laughter. It is a cherished retreat where family and friends gather to pore over the pages of a new book, settling together into the quiet, subterranean comfort of their home.

places.name: Kitchen Table
places.description: The spot where the visiting birds unexpectedly fall asleep after their breakfast.
